Lines on bottles were in 100 mL increments. A full 500mL of water was infiltrated. Do to working on such a steep incline, some water was lost outside of the infiltration ring. < ~25 mL

cur_vegetation: Tsuga heterophylla - Thuja plicata / Vaccinium (alaskaense, ovalifolium) / Polystichum munitum Forest
geo_loc_name: United States: Alaska, Prince of Wales Island, Polk Inlet
infiltration_notes: Mineral soil not present at ground surface; live vegetation/moss and fibric material removed from infiltration site before data collection. Infiltration was collected at a similar starting depth of soil cores, approximately 6 cm below ground surface.
misc_param: Sample set 1 Procheck meter ID: 61412 3B; (name not corrected to 1B on meter log); meter reading taken from mineral soil below E horizon, approximately 40cm below ground surface: Bulk electrical conductivity: 0.058 mS/cm; Water content: 0.454 m3/m3; Temperature 13.0 C
other: Sample set 1 Procheck meter ID: 61412 3A; name not corrected to 1A on meter log / Weather station: Hydaburg Seaplane Base, AK / Soil core collection: Approximately 6 cm of live vegetation and fibric material were removed from the ground surface before core collection; An alternative 30 cm core collection approach was used due to the extensive organics development, rock, and root presence at the site (i.e.,  a soil profile was opened in front of the collection location and live vegetation and fibric materials were removed from the surface, if present. A large soil wedge was cut from the profile using shovels, knives, and cutting shears. Depending on the cohesiveness of the soil, the wedge was excavated either in full or in sections, carefully carved down and fed into the core liner a few centimeters at a time. This approach maintained the spatial structuring and orientation of the sample material).
weather: Light rain, 13 mph wind, 57 F, 93 percent humidity, 1019.1 mb
